calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-02-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Last for and with (administrator) Jaqueline Rogers. Jason Wolford for (creditor) Mary Amen who is present in court.

The Court stated that this is the first and final accounting and asked Mr. Wolford if his client is objecting to it. Mr. Wolford stated no, that they are just here to monitor the proceedings. The Court stated that there are a number of issues, that service on the ex wife has been addressed, that it needs an amendment with proper notice regarding the change in distribution and whether the administrator has explained the request for extraordinary fees. Mr. Last stated that he is not clear regarding the notice on change of distribution, that they are the same but with a little more money, that the Department of Health acknowledged receipt of notice, that they cannot file a claim because there is a surviving spouse, that regarding the extraordinary fees this was not an easy case, that the estate was a mess, that there were 2 abatement issues and that Mary Amen case. Ms. Rogers stated that the hours she wrote down were the hours she had to take off of work. The Court stated that it does not have an accounting for the ordinary services and if statutory fees are enough to compensate for the extraordinary fees requested and that if she wants to claim the time you will need to file a supplement. Mr. Last stated that they are willing to accept the amount at the court's discretion. The Court stated that a response to the examiner's notes was filed but that it should have been an amendment. Mr. Last apologized for the title stating that it was noticed to all heirs. The Court asked if there was additional support for the creditor's claims and that the administrator does not take a position on Bailey's claim. Ms. Rogers stated that she was never informed that she needed to keep time sheets. The Court stated that it will give her an opportunity to submit the information if that is what she wants but that it cannot help with advice given or not given to her. Mr. Wolford stated that his client settled the case in March and that if there are any further hearings the request they be done ASAP. The Accounting petition was taken under submission. The Court stated that it will decide if a more formal amendment is necessary, what to do with the fee request, the adequacy of the creditor's claims support, whether there is a need to wait longer on DHCS and that if there is a need for something additional to be filed the court will contact counsel and put the matter back on calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)
